title: Turn a complaint into a product brief
what_it_does: Converts a raw complaint into a focused user, problem, scope, and demo target.
when_to_use: At sprint start, before choosing features.
prompt_skeleton: Turn this civic complaint into a product brief. Include user, problem, stakes, 48h MVP, non-goals, risks, and demo scenario. Complaint: [text].
verify: Ask a real person with the problem whether the brief feels true.

title: Generate user flows from a brief
what_it_does: Produces step-by-step flows for the main user path.
when_to_use: After the brief, before wireframes or code.
prompt_skeleton: From this brief, produce 3 user flows: first-time user, returning user, and failure path. Keep each to 6 steps. Brief: [text].
verify: Walk through the flow with a timer and a non-team participant.

title: Critique your own idea
what_it_does: Red-teams the concept before the team falls in love with it.
when_to_use: Before committing to the MVP and again before demo.
prompt_skeleton: Act as a skeptical civic technologist. Find the top 10 ways this prototype could fail, mislead users, exclude people, or waste public attention. Then rank the risks by severity.
verify: Convert at least 3 risks into concrete changes or disclaimers.

title: Build a prototype with Lovable, v0, or Bolt
what_it_does: Turns a scoped brief and flow into a fast interface prototype.
when_to_use: When the team needs a working clickable or coded surface quickly.
prompt_skeleton: Build a single-page prototype for [user] to [task]. Use these sections, states, and constraints. Avoid auth. Use accessible HTML. Include sample civic data only.
verify: Run the prototype locally, click every path, and replace fake claims with labels.

title: Debug with Cursor or Claude Code
what_it_does: Uses an AI coding agent to inspect, patch, and test a bug.
when_to_use: When the prototype breaks or behavior does not match the demo script.
prompt_skeleton: Read the repo, identify why [bug] happens, make the smallest fix, and run the relevant test or manual verification. Do not refactor unrelated code.
verify: Review the diff, rerun the broken flow, and write the fix in the build log.

title: PDFs to structured data
what_it_does: Extracts tables, entities, or sections from public PDFs.
when_to_use: For budgets, decisions, procurement documents, reports, and annexes.
prompt_skeleton: Extract this PDF section into CSV with these columns: [columns]. Mark uncertain cells with NEEDS_REVIEW. Do not guess missing values.
verify: Sample 10 rows against the original PDF and record corrections.

title: Landing page in 30 minutes
what_it_does: Produces a simple public page for the project.
when_to_use: After the prototype has a name, user, and demo.
prompt_skeleton: Create a static landing page for this civic prototype. Include problem, user, demo link, source link, limits, and team credits. No analytics or trackers.
verify: Open on mobile width, check links, and run an accessibility pass.

title: Prepare a 5-minute demo
what_it_does: Creates a tight demo script with one live path.
when_to_use: Before finalist selection and public pitch.
prompt_skeleton: Write a 5-minute demo script for this prototype. Include problem, user, live path, what works, what does not, and next ask. No hype.
verify: Rehearse once with a timer and cut 30 percent.

title: Documentation pass
what_it_does: Turns rough notes into a readable README or build log.
prompt_skeleton: Clean this build log without changing facts. Keep uncertainty visible. Preserve prompt excerpts exactly. Output Markdown.
verify: Compare against raw notes and confirm all failures remain visible.

title: Hallucination check
what_it_does: Finds unsupported claims, invented citations, and overconfident summaries.
when_to_use: Before demo, especially for law, money, health, education, or procurement.
prompt_skeleton: List every factual claim in this answer. For each, mark whether it is directly supported by the provided sources. Flag unsupported claims.
verify: Click sources, compare text, and mark unsupported claims in the UI.

title: Non-technical user testing simulation
what_it_does: Simulates confusion from a realistic non-technical user.
prompt_skeleton: Pretend you are [specific user]. Try to use this flow from screenshots/text. Where do you get confused? What words do you not understand?
verify: Test with one real person if possible.

title: Ship-ready accessibility pass
what_it_does: Checks structure, labels, keyboard paths, contrast, and plain-language errors.
prompt_skeleton: Review this HTML/CSS for accessibility issues. Focus on semantics, labels, focus, contrast, keyboard navigation, and mobile readability.
verify: Use keyboard only, run Lighthouse, and fix visible blockers.
